Package model

package model
Contains the core data model classes for the Student QA application.

These classes represent the main domain objects in the system — users, questions, answers, reviews, clarifications, messages, and administrative structures. They serve as the bridge between the database layer (databasePart1) and the application logic (logic) and UI (pages) layers. Each class encapsulates relevant fields, constructors, and getter/setter methods for managing data integrity.

User Roles:

  • User – Base class defining shared attributes and behaviors for all user roles.
  • Admin – Administrative user with system management privileges.
  • Instructor – Instructor-type user with content management permissions.
  • Reviewer – User who provides reviews and assessments on answers.
  • Staff – Staff-type user with elevated access.
  • Student – Standard student user.

Q&A Entities:

  • Question – A question posted by a user, including title, body, and metadata.
  • Questions – Manages CRUD operations and collections of Question objects.
  • Answer – An answer associated with a specific question.
  • Answers – Manages CRUD operations and collections of Answer objects.
  • Review – A reviewer's evaluation of an answer.
  • Reviews – Manages CRUD operations and collections of Review objects.
  • Clarification – A clarification message tied to a question or answer.

Administrative Structures:

  • AdminRequest – A user-submitted request to an administrator.
  • AdminAction – Records an administrative action taken on a request.
  • StaffRequest – A request submitted to or managed by staff.
  • ModerationFlag – Flags content for moderation review.
  • ModerationNote – Notes attached to a moderation flag.

Messaging and Notifications:

  • Messages – Encapsulates private message data exchanged between users.
  • Notification – Represents a notification sent to a user.

Other:

  • ContentType – Enum defining the type of content (e.g., QUESTION, ANSWER).
  • Edits – Tracks edit history for questions or answers.
  • NavigationBar – Defines the application's navigational toolbar UI component.
Author:
CSE360-Team11 Fall 2025
  • Class
    Description
    Admin
    Represents an administrative user with full privileged access in the system.
    AdminAction
    Represents an action taken by an admin on an AdminRequest.
    AdminRequest
    Represents a request submitted by instructors to administrators for system-level actions or interventions.
    AdminRequest.Category
    Available predefined request categories.
    Answer
    Represents a single answer posted to a question.
    Answers
    The Answers class handles all logic related to managing answers, including creation, editing, deletion, and searching.
    Clarification
     
    ContentType
     
    Edits
     
    Instructor
    The Instructor class represents a Instructor entity in the system.
    Messages
     
    ModerationFlag
    Represents a staff-created moderation flag on a question or answer.
    ModerationNote
    Represents an internal note attached to a moderation flag.
    NavigationBar
     
    Notification
    Represents a notification in the system.
    Notification.Type
    Notification types
    Question
    Represents a user-posted question with metadata such as author, title, description, timestamp, tags, and resolution status.
    Questions
     
    Review
    Represents a single review attached to an answer.
    Reviewer
    Represents a reviewer user with permissions to provide feedback and evaluate other users' submissions in the system.
    Reviews
    In-memory manager for Review objects with backing persistence via DatabaseHelper.
    Staff
     
    StaffRequest
    Represents a request from staff members for various actions (e.g., account management, permissions, system changes)
    Student
    Represents a student user with access to questions, answers, and reviews features.
    User
    Represents a generic user in the system, encapsulating common attributes such as ID, username, password, role, name, and email.
    User.Role
